Tafseer:
بَرَاۗءَةٌ مِّنَ اللّٰهِ وَرَسُوْلِهٖٓ اِلَى الَّذِيْنَ عٰهَدْتُّمْ مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِيْنَ

**The Meaning of the Word 'Bara'at':** *Barā'at* means to withdraw from any responsibility and to be free of obligation. Here, it is used in the sense of withdrawing from the responsibility of those treaties which the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) had entered into with various polytheist tribes of Arabia, of both temporary and open-ended nature, after emigrating to Medina. By 'the polytheists' (*al-mushrikīn*) here, and wherever this word is used as a proper noun in the Quran, the polytheists of the Children of Ishmael, or in other words, the polytheists of Arabia, are meant. The reason for this declaration of disassociation from the responsibility of their treaties is made clear ahead in verse 3: many tribes had indeed entered into treaties but were not upholding them with loyalty and uprightness; hence, treaties of this nature were declared null and void, and only the treaties of those tribes who committed no treachery or breach of covenant were maintained, and that too only until their specified term. The preposition *ilā* (to) appearing after the word *barā'at* is proof that the concept of conveyance (*iblāgh*) is also hidden here. That is: the notification of this withdrawal must be conveyed to all the covenant-breaking polytheists, as is also explicitly stated in the subsequent verse.

**A Point Worthy of Attention:** A point worthy of attention here is that as far as entering into a treaty is concerned, its responsibility—as is clear from the word *'āhadttum* (you entered into treaty)—was laid by Allah upon all Muslims, because the responsibility undertaken by the Prophet is the responsibility of all Muslims; but in the matter of being free of obligation, He did not specify the responsibility of the Muslims as to what they must do. This reveals the severity of this declaration of disassociation—that Allah and His Messenger have withdrawn from these weak treaties. Now, let the believers decide for themselves what conduct they must adopt: whether to support Allah and His Messenger to prove their faith, or to adopt the path of hypocrisy out of regard for relative-ship, brotherhood, family, and tribe. The wisdom behind stating the matter in this manner was that in the background, as detailed ahead, there were also hypocrites who were not yet fully free from their familial and tribal bonds. To these people, this style made it clear that this decision from Allah and His Messenger is final, leaving no room for any flexibility; whoever wants to support Him, let them support Him, otherwise let them decide their own path and destination.

فَسِيْحُوْا فِي الْاَرْضِ اَرْبَعَةَ اَشْهُرٍ

**Direct Address to the Polytheists:** While the preceding verse was addressed to the Muslims, the focus of the address in this verse turned directly to the polytheists. This shift of address is proof of the severity of this threat and its decisive nature. Addressing the Muslims, He did not state: convey this threat to the polytheists; but just as He Himself declared the disassociation, He addressed the polytheists directly, stating: now, only four months of respite remain for you, after which there is no responsibility upon Allah and His Messenger for the treaties of those who have violated their covenants; rather, at every step, their apprehension will begin.

**The Wisdoms Behind the Respite of Four Months in the Disassociation of Treaties:** Several wisdoms could be kept in view in the four months of respite. It could be that those who were committing mischief despite the treaty might reconsider their behavior if they wished; it could also be that during this period, Muslims would become completely focused, organized, and prepared for this important step; furthermore, from verse 5, it is also known that the sacred months were approaching, whose respect prevented any military action.

The explanation of 'and know that you cannot cause failure to Allah' passed under Surah Al-Anfal, verse 59.

**General Announcement of Disassociation on the Occasion of Hajj:**

وَاَذَانٌ مِّنَ اللّٰهِ وَرَسُوْلِهٖٓ اِلَى النَّاسِ يَوْمَ الْحَجِّ الْاَكْبَرِ اَنَّ اللّٰهَ بَرِيْقٌ مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِيْنَ ڏ وَرَسُوْلُهٗ ۭ فَاِنْ تُبْتُمْ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۚ وَاِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَاعْلَمُوْٓا اَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِي اللّٰهِ ۭ وَبَشِّرِ الَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا بِعَذَابِ اَلِيْمٍ

'And an announcement' (*adhān*) is used here in its literal sense—meaning: declaration and announcement. Instruction was given that on the Day of the Great Hajj, this announcement be made: Allah and His Messenger are free of obligation from such treaties; now, whoever repents, it is better for him, and those who turn away cannot escape the apprehension of Allah. The instruction to make the announcement on the occasion of Hajj was given because in Arabia, the gathering of Hajj was the only gathering in which people from every corner of the country gathered, because of which whatever statement spread there would spread throughout the entire country. Particularly, since up to the Hajj of 9 AH, the situation was such that the polytheists also went to Hajj, every announcement made there would reach everyone's ears, whether Muslims or disbelievers. For this reason, the general word 'mankind' (*al-nās*) is used in the verse.

**What is Meant by the Great Hajj (al-Hajj al-Akbar)?** What is meant by the Great Hajj, and to which year's Hajj does it point? Commentators answered this question by stating: it refers to the Hajj of 9 AH, which took place under the leadership of Hazrat Abu Bakr Al-Siddiq. In our view, this is correct. This is because this was the first occasion when Muslims was blessed with the regular Hajj. Before this, on the two or three opportunities they found, they could perform only Umrah. Umrah is also called the Lesser Hajj (*al-Hajj al-Asghar*). In comparison to it, the word Great Hajj (*al-Hajj al-Akbar*) was used for the complete Hajj, which gave the Muslims the glad tiding beforehand: up to now, they could perform only the Lesser Hajj, but ahead, they would find the opportunity to be blessed with the Great Hajj.

**The Era of Revelation of Surah At-Tawbah:** From this, people have generally deduced that this Surah was revealed in the year 9 AH, but I hesitate to accept this deduction because the upcoming verses, as you will see, clearly show that at least these and the subsequent verses were revealed prior to the termination of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and the Conquest of Mecca. However, since the general announcement of disassociation was made by the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) on the occasion of the Hajj of 9 AH, some assumed that these verses were also revealed on that occasion. In reality, this was a pre-notification that when the opportunity to perform the Great Hajj arrived, this decision must be announced generally on that occasion. This also gave the Muslims, as we indicated, the glad tiding of performing the Hajj.

For me, defining the exact era of the revelation of these verses is difficult. This is because in Arabia, up to the Farewell Pilgrimage, two calendars were in use: one was based on the simple lunar calculation, and the second was based on that rule of intercalation (*nasī'*) to which reference is coming ahead in this Surah. If someone can calculate the difference the rule of intercalation caused in the original lunar months, they can define those four months mentioned in verse 2, and identify those sacred months referred to in verse 5. In this research, major guidance can be obtained from the accepted reality that the Hajj the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) performed in 10 AH fell exactly on the 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah according to the lunar calculation, and was also on this date according to the intercalation calculation—meaning: both calendars had aligned. In the Farewell Pilgrimage, the Prophet pointed to this reality: 'Time has completed its cycle, returning to its original state on the day Allah created the heavens and the earth.' I am not a man of calculations; therefore, stating anything with certainty in this regard is difficult for me, but in the light of the Quran's words and its structure, I am satisfied that these verses were revealed shortly before the termination of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah. The details of the proofs will come.

اِلَّا الَّذِيْنَ عٰهَدْتُّمْ مِّنَ الْمُشْرِكِيْنَ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَنْقُصُوْكُمْ شَيْــــًٔـا وَّلَمْ يُظَاهِرُوْا عَلَيْكُمْ اَحَدًا فَاَتِمُّــوْٓا اِلَيْهِمْ عَهْدَهُمْ اِلٰى مُدَّتِهِمْ ۭ اِنَّ اللّٰهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَّقِيْنَ 

**The Polytheists Excluded From the Declaration of Disassociation:** This describes those polytheists who were excluded from the aforementioned declaration. These are those tribes who remained firm on their treaty, committing no action contrary to the treaty, nor helping anyone against the Muslims. This made it clear that the aforementioned declaration related solely to the covenant-breakers, but simultaneously, it also reveals that these treaties were permitted to be maintained only until their specified term. After the term expired, they also became null and void. For the future, entering into any new treaty with them was not permitted. Consequently, after this declaration of disassociation, the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) did not enter into any treaty with any group of the polytheists. The reason is that the treaties entered into with these polytheists were made solely for the sake of calling, reminding, and completing the proof. The purpose was not to maintain relations with them forever. This divine law we have explained in more than one place: whenever a messenger is sent directly to a nation, if they persist in denying the messenger after calling, reminding, and completing the proof, they are inevitably destroyed—whether through His physical punishment or through the swords of the believers. The case of the polytheists of Arabia was of this nature. Now, the era of calling, reminding, and completing the proof was ending for them; therefore, entering into any treaty with any group of theirs was out of the question.

**Clarification of Collective Piety:** In 'Indeed, Allah loves those who fear Him' (*Inna Allāha yuhibbu al-muttaqīn*), the 'piety' (*taqwa*) mentioned is not individual piety, but collective and political piety. Just as Islam demands individual piety from everyone, it demands collective and political piety from the Muslims as a community. That is: when Muslims enter into transactions and treaties with other nations, they must remain upright, truthful, and loyal, committing no minor violation of any treaty or promise. God loves such upright ones, and those loved by God are prosperous and successful in this world and the Hereafter.

فَاِذَا انْسَلَخَ الْاَشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِيْنَ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُّـمُوْهُمْ وَخُذُوْهُمْ وَاحْصُرُوْهُمْ وَاقْعُدُوْا لَهُمْ كُلَّ مَرْصَدٍ ۚ فَاِنْ تَابُوْا وَاَقَامُوا الصَّلٰوةَ وَاٰتَوُا الزَّكٰوةَ فَخَــلُّوْا سَـبِيْلَهُمْ ۭاِنَّ اللّٰهَ غَفُوْرٌ رَّحِيْمٌ

**'The Sacred Months':** 'The sacred months' refers to Dhu al-Qa'dah, Dhu al-Hijjah, Muharram, and Rajab. 'The sacred months' (*al-ashhur al-hurum*) is used as a proper noun for these months. There is no room to interpret any other month through this word. These months had been held sacred since the times of ignorance, and indeed since the time of Abraham (peace be upon him). We explained in the commentary of Surah Al-Baqarah that these were the months of Hajj and Umrah, and the bulk of the commercial and economic movements of the Arabs occurred in these months. Fighting was prohibited in them, and the Arabs respected this prohibition despite their warrior nature. If you add Shawwal to the three consecutive sacred months, they make up the four months of respite mentioned in verse 2. He stated: When the sacred months have passed, kill the polytheists wherever you find them. 'Wherever you find them' (*haythu wajadtumūhum*) means, as explained in Surah Al-Baqarah, that fighting them is permitted even within the Sanctuary.

**Apprehending the Polytheists of Arabia:** 'and capture them and besiege them and sit in wait for them at every place of ambush.' That is: take every military action against them, and close every path for them. The reason for this severe apprehension is that its nature was not merely an action against an enemy, but was the manifestation of that divine law which has always appeared for nations who denied their messengers, details of which were described in Surah Al-A'raf.

'But if they should repent, establish prayer, and give zakat, then let them go on their way.' **For the Polytheists of Arabia, Only Two Paths: Islam or the Sword:** The meaning is: this apprehension must not be stopped until they repent of their disbelief and polytheism, establish prayer, and pay zakat. Prayer and zakat is a comprehensive expression for entering the Islamic system of worship and obedience, which means that without Islam, there remained no room for them to live in the Islamic state as subjects (*dhimmi*) or allies, nor as male or female slaves. Only two paths remained for them: either accepting Islam or the sword.

**The Reason for This Specific Treatment of the Polytheists of Arabia:** The reason for this specific treatment is what we pointed to above: Allah sent among them a messenger from themselves, revealed His Book in their language, and invited them to the original religion of Abraham. For 23 years, the Messenger shook and woke them, reaching every gathering of theirs, knocking on every door. He answered their every doubt and objection, countering all their slanders, accusations, and enmities. Upon their demand, he showed them miracles, proving himself true on their chosen scales. Finally, when the good among them became his companions, those who still refused to accept the truth after all this preparation did not deserve to be spared. A messenger represents the highest and final stage of completing the proof; those who do not wake through him are dead, and for the dead, it is best to be buried.

**A Final Concession to Complete the Proof:** While the preceding verse commanded the apprehension of the polytheists, this is a final concession to complete the proof. He stated: 'And if any one of the polytheists seeks your protection, then grant him protection so that he may hear the words of Allah. Then deliver him to his place of safety.' If anyone seeks protection during this apprehension, deliver him to a place of safety so he may reflect on his matter with an open mind, deciding whether to accept Islam or the sword. This protection, as we indicated, was granted solely to complete the proof. It did not mean he was spared from the command of apprehension. If he does not accept faith, the same command mentioned above remains for him, and when he falls under apprehension again, this protection will not benefit him, nor will there be any room for him to seek protection again. 'That is because they are a people who do not know' explains the reason for this concession: since they are unlettered bards, ignorant of the Shariah, and unfamiliar with prophethood and message, it is possible that despite this long period of calling, some aspect of completing the proof remained incomplete; and since the command of apprehension, as we indicated, was based on completing the proof, He instructed that if anyone seeks protection, grant it to him. Although there was a danger that someone might exploit this concession for espionage or to return as an opponent.

**The Reasons for the Declaration of Disassociation:** This describes the reasons and proofs for the declaration of disassociation. Although the address is outwardly general, the focus is on those Muslims who were in anxiety regarding this declaration due to their relationships and ties with the polytheists. This declaration made it clear to everyone that the days of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah—which had opened the way for Muslims and the Quraysh to meet—were drawing to a close. For those weak in faith, their faith fell into a severe test. They had relatives in every home of the Quraysh, expecting that this door of meeting would remain open and they would continue to maintain ties with both disbelief and Islam. This declaration ended this expectation forever, showing them that the time was coming when they must draw their swords against their own relatives. Addressing those of this mind, He stated: How can there be any responsibility upon Allah and His Messenger for the treaties of those who, if they gain dominance over you, respect neither relative-ship nor covenant? Through words, they wish to please you, but their hearts boil with anger and hatred against you; the majority of them are covenant-breakers.

**The Structure of Verses 7-8:** The structure of this verse is worth noting: He began the statement with 'How can there be for the polytheists a treaty with Allah and with His Messenger,' but before completing the statement, He introduced an exception as a parenthetical clause—namely: 'except for those with whom you made a treaty at the Sacred Mosque? So as long as they are upright toward you, be upright toward them.' Then, He recalled the statement left incomplete because of this parenthetical clause, beginning it with the same introduction to complete it.

**An Indication to the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah:** 'How can there be for the polytheists a treaty with Allah and with His Messenger, except for those with whom you made a treaty at the Sacred Mosque? So as long as they are upright toward you, be upright toward them. Indeed, Allah loves those who fear Him.' In our view, this refers to that treaty which was entered into with the Quraysh on the occasion of Hudaybiyyah. The proofs for this view will come ahead, but one indication is worth noting here: He identified it through 'those with whom you made a treaty at the Sacred Mosque' (*al-laḏīna ʿāhadttum ʿinda al-Masǧidi al-Ḥarām*). Of all the treaties the Prophet entered into with the polytheists after emigrating, the only treaty that deserves to be identified through its association with the Sacred Mosque is the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, as this treaty was entered into in the vicinity of the Sacred Mosque. Expressing this association served to identify the treaty, while highlighting its extraordinary sanctity—showing that this was no ordinary treaty, but its completion occurred near the Sacred Mosque, than which no place can be more sacred and respected.

**Instruction to Abide by the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah:** He stated: 'So as long as they are upright toward you, be upright toward them'—meaning: as long as the Quraysh abide by this treaty, you also abide by it. If they break it, you also break it. No treaty remains one-sided; both sides must maintain it. This is the requirement of justice, and Islam commands you to do this. 'Indeed, Allah loves those who fear Him'—that is: Allah loves such lovers of justice.

**Three Points Regarding Treaties:** From the preceding verses, three points were clarified regarding all those treaties entered into between the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) and various groups of the polytheists up to that time:

1. Regarding those polytheists who violated their treaties, He declared the disassociation, and after the four months of respite, commanded war against them.
2. Regarding those who upheld their treaties with loyalty, and their treaties were temporary, these treaties were also to end after the expiration of their term.

**The Specific Character of the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah:** 3. Instruction was given to maintain the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah as long as the Quraysh maintained it. It should be remembered that the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was open-ended, and up to the revelation of these verses, the Quraysh were maintaining it in some manner; therefore, a question must have arisen in many minds: what would be its fate after the expiration of the four months? This is the answer to that question. It seems that during this period, the Quraysh violated this treaty by helping their allies, the Banu Bakr, against the Prophet's allies, the Banu Khuza'ah, as a result of which the Prophet launched an attack against Mecca and conquered it.

He stated: 'How [can there be a treaty] while, if they gain dominance over you, they do not regard concerning you consanguinity or covenant?'

**The Foundations of Social and Political Relations:** Relations depend on two things: social relations depend on respecting the bonds of womb and relative-ship, and political relations depend on respecting the responsibilities imposed by mutual treaties. The first is expressed through 'consanguinity' (*ill*), which points to those rights that arise naturally between those of the same origin, relative-ship, or neighborhood. The second is expressed through 'covenant' (*ḏimmah*), which points to those responsibilities laid upon the parties of a treaty by virtue of that treaty. He stated: Let no one among you be in anxiety regarding this declaration of disassociation, thinking that all social and collective relations with them are ending. If they are ending, they had to end. Relations never remain one-sided. Those of you who value these relations must understand that if they ever gain dominance over you, they will respect neither relative-ship nor treaty. The sweet words they utter when meeting you are merely a verbal display of sympathy to deceive you, while their hearts do not align with their tongues. Their hearts are filled with enmity and malice against you and your religion.

'and most of them are defiantly disobedient'—**The Meaning of 'Fisq' (Defiantly Disobedient):** That is: just as their relative-ship is merely verbal and for show, they are false and treacherous regarding their treaties. The majority of them are covenant-breakers. The word 'defiantly disobedient' (*fāsiqūn*) here is used in the sense of treachery and breach of covenant, which is a well-known usage of this word in the Quran. Just as the word 'piety' (*taqwa*) was used above in the sense of keeping covenants, 'defiantly disobedient' is used here in the sense of breaking covenants.

Commentary on Verses 9-11:

اِشْتَرَوْا بِاٰيٰتِ اللّٰهِ ثَـمَنًا قَلِيْلًا فَصَدُّوْا عَنْ سَبِيْلِهٖ ۭاِنَّهُمْ سَاۗءَ مَا كَانُوْا يَعْمَلُوْنَ ۔ لَا يَرْقُبُوْنَ فِيْ مُؤْمِنٍ اِلًّا وَّلَا ذِمَّةً ۭ وَاُولٰۗىِٕكَ هُمُ الْمُعْتَدُوْنَ ۔ فَاِنْ تَابُوْا وَاَقَامُوا الصَّلٰوةَ وَاٰتَوُا الزَّكٰوةَ فَاِخْوَانُكُمْ فِي الدِّيْنِ ۭوَنُفَصِّلُ الْاٰيٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَّعْلَمُوْنَ 

**Further Clarification of the Reasons for Disassociation:** This is further clarification of the reasons why these polytheists do not deserve any treaty with Allah and His Messenger: their dealing is correct neither with the Creator nor with creation. When God's guidance descended to them, they preferred the petty goods of this world over it, turning away from it themselves and blocking others from it. Their dealing with creation is: in the case of a Muslim, they respect neither relative-ship nor covenant. 'And those are the transgressors' (*wa-ulā'ika humu al-mu'tadūna*)—that is: they are the ones who initiated the violation of rights and breaking of limits; how then can any treaty remain between Allah and His Messenger and such covenant-breakers and wrongdoers? Now, the only path for them is to repent, establish prayer, and pay zakat, in which case they will become your brothers in religion. 'And We detail the verses for a people who know' contains a warning for both the Muslims and the disbelievers. The meaning is: for those who wish to know and understand, Allah's commands on this subject have been explained with full clarity, leaving no room for confusion. Now, if any Muslim attempts to deal with these polytheists contrary to this, the responsibility lies on him; and if any polytheist expects anything contrary to this, the responsibility also lies on him.

Commentary on Verses 12-15:

وَاِنْ نَّكُثُوْٓا اَيْمَانَهُمْ مِّنْۢ بَعْدِ عَهْدِهِمْ وَطَعَنُوْا فِيْ دِيْنِكُمْ فَقَاتِلُوْٓا اَىِٕمَّةَ الْكُفْرِ ۙ اِنَّهُمْ لَآ اَيْمَانَ لَهُمْ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَنْتَهُوْنَ ۔ اَلَا تُقَاتِلُوْنَ قَوْمًا نَّكَتُثْوا اَيْمَانَهُمْ وَهَمُّوْا بِاِخْرَاجِ الرَّسُوْلِ وَهُمْ بَدَءُوْكُمْ اَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۭ اَتَخْشَوْنَهُمْ ۚ فَاللّٰهُ اَحَقُّ اَنْ تَخْشَوْهُ اِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُّؤْمِنِيْنَ ۔ قَاتِلُوْهُمْ يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللّٰهُ بِاَيْدِيْكُمْ وَيُخْزِهِمْ وَيَنْصُرْكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَيَشْفِ صُدُوْرَ قَوْمٍ مُّؤْمِنِيْنَ ۔ وَيُذْهِبْ غَيْظَ قُلُوْبِهِمْ ۭ وَيَتُوْبُ اللّٰهُ عَلٰي مَنْ يَّشَاۗءُ ۭوَاللّٰهُ عَلِيْمٌ حَكِيْمٌ

**Instructions Regarding the Quraysh:**

'And if they break their oaths after their treaty and defame your religion, then fight the leaders of disbelief.' This relates to verse 7 above, where He stated: as long as they are upright toward you, be upright toward them. Now, He stated: if the Quraysh break their treaty, fight these leaders of disbelief. In Arabia, 'the leaders of disbelief' (*a'immat al-kufr*) could apply to none other than the Quraysh, as the entire peninsula followed them in religion, and they possessed the leadership and authority. Using this title for the Quraysh also carries a strong argument for fighting them: when they are the very leaders and ringleaders of all disbelief, if you do not fight them, whom will you fight? All the slanders, mockeries, and propaganda against Islam were developed in their workshops and then spread to others. This is what is pointed to in 'and defame your religion' (*wa-ta'anū fī dīnikum*). 'for indeed, there are no oaths [sacred] to them' is a parenthetical clause, and 'that they might cease' (*la'allahum yantahūna*) is connected to the core sequence. The meaning is: their treaties carry no weight; they will not maintain them for long, but will break them sooner or later; whenever they break them, fight them so they desist from their mischief. The meaning of their desisting was clarified in the preceding verses—namely: that they repent and accept Islam.

**Instruction to Fight the Quraysh After They Broke the Treaty:** 'Would you not fight a people who broke their oaths and determined to expel the Messenger...' This encourages the Muslims to fight the Quraysh, and the context indicates that this verse was revealed after some interval: while the preceding verse used 'if they break' (*wa-in nakathū*), which shows that the treaty was still formally intact, this verse used 'they broke' (*nakathū*), which shows that they had practically broken the treaty. The form of this breach, as we indicated, was that the Quraysh helped their allies against the Prophet's allies.

**The Crimes of the Quraysh:** 'and determined to expel the Messenger'—alongside the crime of breach of treaty, He referenced some of their past crimes, particularly those they committed against the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) and the Muslims. Among these, the most serious crime was determining to exile the Messenger. This crime is of such gravity that, as explained in previous Surahs, it brings a decisive punishment. The meaning is: will you show cowardice in fighting those who, according to the law of God, deserve punishment? This crime is expressed in 'they determined to expel the Messenger' (*wa-hammū bi-ihrāǧi al-rasūl*), which shows that their crime was determining to exile him. This determination itself is a great crime. 'and they began [hostility against] you the first time'—not only did they determine to exile him, but they also initiated the fighting. Obviously, this points to the Battle of Badr, which was discussed in detail in Al-Anfal. These words also support what we clarified previously: that the advance for the Battle of Badr was entirely from the Quraysh, who used the excuse of protecting their caravan.

It should noted that these crimes were fundamentally the crimes of the Quraysh; therefore, these verses can relate only to the Quraysh. This supports our view regarding the era of the revelation of this Surah.

**Warning to the Weaker Muslims:** 'Do you fear them? But Allah has more right that you should fear Him, if you should be believers.' Although the address is general, the focus is on those weaker and hypocritical people who feared fighting the Quraysh, and the reason for this fear, more than the battle itself, was that they valued their past relationships and ties. Their hearts were not ready to accept that these relationships would end. He stated: Do not fear people, but fear Allah, who is more deserving of your fear. If you claim faith, you must care for your relationship with Allah more than your relationships with others.

**Glad Tidings of the Enemy's Disgrace in the Clash:** 'Fight them; Allah will punish them by your hands and will disgrace them and give you victory over them.' This encourages the Muslims, especially the weaker ones: do not show weakness in fighting these leaders of disbelief; Allah has decided to punish them and disgrace them through your hands. We mentioned this divine law previously: when a nation persists in denying its messenger, after his emigration, He punishes them. If those believing in the messenger are very few, this punishment comes directly from God; but if the believers are a substantial number, He punishes and disgraces that nation through the hands of those very believers. This disgrace is a specific part of the punishment, because the cause of denying the messenger is arrogance, and the punishment for arrogance is disgrace.

**Consolation to the Oppressed Muslims:** 'and satisfy the breasts of a believing people'—this consoles those Muslims who, because of being helpless, had been subjected to cruel oppressions by the wicked and hard-hearted of the Quraysh in Mecca. He stated: Allah will humiliate and disgrace these hard-hearted ones through your hands in such an exemplary manner that seeing their end will satisfy the hearts of those who were persecuted by these wretched ones for embracing Islam. It should noted that harboring anger against the enemies of truth and being delighted by their destruction is completely permissible for those who have completed the proof against them, performing their duty.

**Glad Tidings to Those Desiring the Faith of Their Relatives:** 'And remove the anger of their hearts. And Allah accepts repentance of whom He wills; and Allah is Knowing and Wise.' In 'And Allah accepts repentance of whom He wills' (*wa-yatūbu Allāhu ʿalā man yašā'u*), there is a subtle indication that some among them will be blessed with the facilitation of repentance, embracing Islam. This is a glad tiding to those Muslims who desired the faith of their relatives. He stated: Be assured; if there are any jewels hidden in this rubbish, Allah will select them. Through 'of whom He wills' and 'Allah is Knowing and Wise,' He pointed to that divine law which He chooses in granting the facilitation of repentance, which was explained in multiple places in this book.

اَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ اَنْ تُتْرَكُوْا وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ اللّٰهُ الَّذِيْنَ جٰهَدُوْا مِنْكُمْ وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذُوْا مِنْ دُوْنِ اللّٰهِ وَلَا رَسُوْلِهٖ وَلَا الْمُؤْمِنِيْنَ وَلِيْجَةً ۭ وَاللّٰهُ خَبِيْرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُوْنَ 

*Walīǧah* means an intimate, close friend, or trustee whom one takes from outside one's own people.

Although the address is general in terms of words, the focus is on those Muslims who were in anxiety regarding this declaration due to their relationships with the polytheists, because of which this declaration became a severe test for them. Addressing them, He stated: This declaration is a criterion to distinguish between your sincere and insincere—showing who are those strivings in His cause, completely free of any regard for relationships in opposition to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers, and who are those who would ignore Allah, His Messenger, and the believers for the sake of others. This test is a divine law through which those who claim faith must pass. Although Allah is aware of everyone's deeds, His law is to test them on the criterion to distinguish the good from the bad and purify the believers. If you assumed you would be left alone after claiming faith, this was wrong; the stage of your testing has arrived—showing who is loyal to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers, and who is a false claimant.

**The Enemy of the Believers is the Enemy of Allah and His Messenger:** It should noted that Allah, His Messenger, and the believers are mentioned together, which points to the reality that there is no room for division here. Whoever claims to be loyal to Allah must prove himself loyal to His Messenger and those who believe in him. If someone takes anyone else as an ally in opposition to the believers, he is not a companion of Allah and His Messenger, no matter how loudly he claims it. This subject is detailed ahead in verses 23-24.

The meaning of *'alima/ya'lamu* was discussed elsewhere.

Commentary on Verses 17-22:

**Verse 17:** مَا كَانَ لِلْمُشْرِكِيْنَ اَنْ يَّعْمُرُوْا مَسٰجِدَ اللّٰهِ شٰهِدِيْنَ عَلٰٓي اَنْفُسِهِمْ بِالْكُفْرِ ۭاُولٰۗىِٕكَ حَبِطَتْ اَعْمَالُهُمْ ښ وَفِي النَّارِ هُمْ خٰلِدُوْنَ 

**A Partial Good Deed Cannot Stand in Place of the Core Purpose:** In the preceding paragraph, those crimes of the Quraysh which made them deserve the declaration of disassociation and war were mentioned. Now, He referred to some of their services in guarding the Kaaba, clarifying that these services are completely valueless and carry no weight, and they do not deserve any favor because of them. Any service or good deed is valid only when accompanied by protecting the core purpose. If the core purpose is destroyed, a partial good deed cannot stand in its place. If the guardian of a mosque turns the mosque into a temple of idols, he does not deserve any credit merely because he arranged water in the mosque. Arranging water is a good deed in itself, but it is not such that his right to keep the mosque as a temple of idols and remain its guardian should be accepted because of it.

It should noted that the Quraysh were proud of these services, and their other sympathizers also considered these deeds worthy of regard. Particularly, when war was declared against them, those Muslims who were not yet fully focused began thinking: these people are the guardians of the Kaaba, they serve it and provide water to pilgrims; therefore, they deserve some favor, and we must not deal with them so harshly as to offer them only Islam or the sword. This was a corrupt mind which provided an excuse for those who denied the Messenger, while allowing a highly bad form of hypocrisy to grow; therefore, the Quran corrected this wrong concept so it would not take root within the Muslims.

'It is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of Allah while they witness against themselves disbelief.' 'To maintain' (*'amara/ya'muru*) means to construct, populate, manage, or administer a place. Although 'the mosques of Allah' (*masāǧid Allāh*) refers to the Sacred Mosque, as is clarified in verse 19, it is expressed through the plural to show that the case of the Sacred Mosque is the case of all mosques of Allah. This is the origin, center, and Qiblah of all mosques. If any corruption arises in its administration, purpose, and call, it means that the center of all guidance and blessing is thrown into chaos.

He stated: The polytheists have no right to manage the Sacred Mosque—the center and Qiblah of all mosques of Allah—while they witness against themselves disbelief. 'Disbelief' (*kufr*) here refers to their polytheism. By expressing polytheism through the word *kufr*, this reality of religion was made clear: believing in God alongside associating partners with Him is equivalent to denying Him. Only that belief in God is valid which is accompanied by monotheism; particularly with this polytheism which is openly declared and displayed, there is no possibility of belief in Allah. It should be kept in mind that for the polytheists of Arabia, polytheism was not merely a logical consequence of their words or actions, but was adopted by them as their religion and belief; it was an inseparable part of their concept of divinity. Obviously, those who are the standard-bearers of this disbelief have no right to dominate the guardianship of this House—the very first house of monotheism on earth and the Qiblah of all mosques of Allah. Their managing this House is no good deed to serve as an intercession for them, but is a great evil from which the believers must purify this House as their primary duty.

**Every Good Deed Goes to Waste Alongside Polytheism:** 'Those - their deeds have become worthless, and in the Fire they will abide eternally.' 'Their deeds' refers to those deeds which people counted as good and as services to the religion. He stated: all their deeds will go to waste, and they will abide eternally in Hell. Alongside polytheism, no good deed remains a good deed. Before God, only that good deed remains which is accompanied by monotheism. In religious scriptures, the polytheist is compared to an adulterous wife: just as all her love and affection for her husband is useless if she is unfaithful, all the deeds of a servant are wasted if he associates partners with his Lord.

**The True Guardians of the Mosques of Allah:** 'The mosques of Allah are only to be maintained by those who believe in Allah and the Last Day and establish prayer and give zakat and do not fear except Allah.' Now, He described who the true guardians of the Kaaba and mosques of Allah are. He stated: Only those who believe in Allah and the Last Day, establish prayer, and pay zakat are entitled to it. Obviously, those characterized by these attributes were the Muslims, not the polytheists. But instead of naming the Muslims, He mentioned only those attributes which represent the fundamental conditions for the office of guardianship, so it would be clear that this office is not the monopoly of any group or family, but is conditional upon these attributes and duties. If these are missing, no group can claim this office. 'and do not fear except Allah' is to negate polytheism—meaning: no fear of any other than Allah exists within them. Obviously, fear here refers to that fear which arises from believing that someone other than Allah is beneficial or harmful in himself. This fear is polytheism. He negated polytheism by negating its primary motive. We explained in our book *The Reality of Polytheism* that the primary cause of polytheism is this fear.

**The Successful Group:** 'for it is expected that those will be of the [rightly] guided.' 'Guidance' (*ihtidā'*) here is used in the sense of guidance to the destination. That is: those characterized by these attributes are expected to reach the destination and succeed. This way of expressing the matter through the word *'asā* (expected) is an indication that this path is not easy, but is filled with difficulties and trials. Only those can remain steadfast on the straight path who possess the provision of divine facilitation and help.

**The Polytheists' Services to the Kaaba are Fruitless:** 'Have you made the providing of water to the pilgrim and the maintenance of the Sacred Mosque like [the deeds of] one who believes in Allah and the Last Day and strives in the cause of Allah?' In 'like [the deeds of] one who believes' (*ka-man āmana*), a noun is omitted in the genitive construction, just as in 'but righteousness is [in] one who believes.' The addressees are those who, because of the Quraysh's guardianship and services like providing water to pilgrims, gave them a status of distinction, considering them deserving of favor. He stated: Can providing water to pilgrims and managing the Sacred Mosque stand in place of believing in Allah and the Last Day and striving in His cause? 'They are not equal in the sight of Allah'—you may consider these deeds equal, but before God, these two kinds of people are not equal. 'And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people'—God will not make these polytheists successful. 'Wrongdoing' (*thulm*) here refers to polytheism, and 'guidance' refers to guidance to the destination. We explained above that those deeds performed alongside polytheism are like drawing on water; before God, they will go to waste, and all hopes attached to them will end in failure.

**Verse 20:** 'Those who believed and emigrated and strove in the cause of Allah with their wealth and their lives are greater in degree in the sight of Allah.' 'Greater in degree' (*aʿẓamu daraǧatan*) here is not used for comparison, but to highlight their great status. The meaning is: the rank of those who believed, emigrated, and strove is highly elevated before Allah, just as in Surah Al-Baqarah: 'and those who fear Him will be above them on the Day of Resurrection.' (Al-Baqarah: 212). In this style, the greatness of the believers' rank is described in itself, without regard to the disbelievers, whose fate was made clear in 'Those - their deeds have become worthless, and in the Fire they will abide eternally.' 'And it is those who are the attainers [of success]' stands in direct opposition to 'And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people'—meaning: the polytheists will fail in the Hereafter, but the believers will succeed.

**Verses 21-22:** 'Their Lord gives them good tidings of mercy from Him and approval and of gardens for them wherein is enduring pleasure. Abiding therein eternally, forever. Indeed, Allah has with Him a great reward.' This is the explanation of 'And it is those who are the attainers [of success]': in return for their faith, emigration, and Jihad, their Lord gives them good tidings of His mercy, approval, and gardens of enduring pleasure. Unlike worldly blessings, these blessings do not face decline.

Commentary on Verses 23-24:

**Verse 23:** 'O you who have believed, do not take your fathers or your brothers as allies if they have preferred disbelief over belief.'

**The Attributes of True Believers:** In verse 16 above, the subject passed that Allah does not tolerate having those within the ranks of the believers who claim faith but bfriend the disbelievers. Those who possess true faith are only those who strive in His cause, taking no ally in opposition to Allah, His Messenger, and the believers. Now, this same subject is stated in another style, with a highly clear and decisive tone, before all Muslims, so everyone may choose their path and destination.

He stated: The foundation of relationship, friendship, and trust among you is faith, not lineage and family. If your fathers and brothers prefer disbelief over faith, do not take them as allies and trustees. Whoever does so must remember that he wrongs his own soul: 'And whoever does so among you - then it is those who are the wrongdoers' (*fa-ulā'ika humu al-thālimūna*)—meaning: the punishment that falls upon him in this world and the Hereafter because of this will be of his own doing; there will be no injustice from anyone else in it.

**Verse 24:** 'Say, [O Muhammad], "If your fathers, your sons, your brothers, your wives, your relatives, wealth which you have obtained, commerce wherein you fear decline, and dwellings with which you are pleased are more beloved to you than Allah and His Messenger and jihad in His cause, then wait until Allah delivers His command. And Allah does not guide the defiantly disobedient people."

This is the final warning on this subject, expressed in a highly decisive manner. Listing every single thing desirable to man, He stated: if any of these is more beloved to someone than Allah, His Messenger, and striving in His cause, let him wait for the decision of Allah.

**What it Means to Love Allah and His Messenger More:** Having something more beloved than Allah and His Messenger means: when faced with two conflicting demands—on one side, the demand of Allah and His Messenger, and on the other, the demand of the love of these things—a person ignores the demand of Allah and His Messenger to prefer the other; and if, in contrast, he prioritizes the demand of Allah and His Messenger over the other, it means he has preferred the love of Allah and His Messenger. This love is the indispensable requirement of faith. Without it, no claim to faith is valid, and this is a criterion to test love and faith, through which everyone can test their faith and love in their daily life.

**Desirable Things of the Soul and the Demand of Faith:** The list of desirable things mentioned here possesses a highly subtle psychological order which is obvious in itself. First He mentioned the fathers, sons, brothers, wives, and family, whose love and bias can become a veil and trial for a person; then He mentioned the wealth, commerce, and dwellings, which are desired fundamentally in connection with these relatives, and become a trial if a person is not blessed with facilitation. Alongside wealth, the restriction 'which you have obtained' (*iqtaraftumūhā*) is used. *Iqtirāf* means earning. This restriction highlights how beloved this wealth is. The custom is: the wealth a person earned and increased himself is more beloved to him. Similarly, alongside commerce, the restriction 'wherein you fear decline' (*takhšawna kasādahā*) points to the fact that this is a successful and active commerce, because it is only regarding successful commerce that the merchant fears decline, doing everything to protect it until it becomes his deity—so that neither the distinction of lawful and unlawful remains for him, nor the trials of emigration, Jihad, and cutting ties are tolerable to him. He stated: every such thing is an idol, and until a servant is ready to break every such idol for the sake of Allah, he cannot fulfill the requirements of faith.

**The Fate of the False Claimants to Faith:** 'then wait until Allah delivers His command' is a threat, and a highly severe one. Within its ambiguity lies a detailed meaning. The fate of those who denied the Messenger was described above. Here, He pointed to the same: let those claimants to faith who wish to ride both the boat of disbelief and that of faith prepared to face their evil end.

'And Allah does not guide the defiantly disobedient people.' Just as He stated regarding the polytheists 'And Allah does not guide the wrongdoing people,' He stated regarding those who are hesitant between disbelief and Islam that Allah will not guide such defiantly disobedient ones. 'Defiantly disobedient' (*fāsiqūn*) here is used in the sense of exiting from faith, which reveals that those who take the disbelievers as allies in opposition to Allah and His Messenger exit from faith despite their claim.

Commentary on Verses 25-27:

**Verse 25:** 'Allah has already given you victory in many regions and [even] on the day of Hunayn, when your great number pleased you, but it did not avail you at all, and the earth was vast as it is, was straitened for you; then you turned back in flight.'

**Encouraging the Weaker Ones:** 'Allah has already given you victory in many regions' was stated to encourage those hesitant ones: do not hesitate to fight them, for the God who helped you on many occasions is still with you. After emigration and up to the Conquest of Mecca, multiple battles had been fought with the polytheists, in almost all of which Allah granted the Muslims victory. Referencing all of them, He stated: place your trust in God whose help has always accompanied you; He will not deprive you of His help.

**Consolation to the Weaker Ones Using the Battle of Hunayn as an Example:** 'and [even] on the day of Hunayn, when your great number pleased you, but it did not avail you at all...' This answers a doubt that could arise in weaker minds. Shortly before this, in the Battle of Hunayn, Muslims had faced a temporary defeat in the beginning. For this reason, it was necessary to explain the true nature of this incident. He stated: on the Day of Hunayn, too, Allah helped you, though in the beginning, your numerical majority produced a wrong self-confidence and indifference within you, which brought you a defeat and you fled from the battlefield; but then, Allah sent down resolve and courage upon His Messenger and the sincere believers, sending His unseen armies to help you, and crushing the disbelievers through your hands.

**The Cause of the Initial Defeat at the Battle of Hunayn:** The meaning is: Allah granted you victory in this battle too, though after warning you of your error. In the Battle of Hunayn, narrations show that the Muslims' army consisted of 12,000 Mujahideen. Before this, they had defeated massive armies of the disbelievers with small numbers and ordinary weapons. When they went out as a massive army for the first time, this thought must have arisen in their hearts: who can stand against us today? This wrong self-confidence naturally produced indifference within them. Many were deprived of that focus toward Allah commanded in Surah Al-Anfal, verse 45: 'when you face a group, remain steadfast and remember Allah much, so you may succeed.' They also lacked that care for organization, discipline, obedience, and sincerity commanded in verses 46-47: 'and obey Allah and His Messenger and do not dispute and [thus] lose courage and then your strength depart; and be patient; indeed, Allah is with the patient. And do not be like those who came forth from their homes insolently and to be seen by the people...' Consequently, just as the violation of the Prophet's command and disputing at Uhud brought defeat, the pride of numbers at Hunayn brought danger, because within this pride lay that insolence and show which is disliked by Allah, and from which He warned those who go out in His cause. This pride makes a person rely on means and resources instead of Allah, whereas a believer's reliance must be on Allah under all circumstances. If reliance on Allah is weak, the largest numbers and the most resources become like rubbish, and those who boasted that no one could stand against them find that the earth, with all its vastness, is straitened for them, so they cannot see any way to escape. 'but it did not avail you at all, and the earth was vast as it is, was straitened for you' is a picture of this state.

**Verse 26:** 'Then Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Messenger and upon the believers and sent down soldiers which you did not see and punished those who disbelieved. And that is the recompense of the disbelievers.'

**Victory After Defeat in the Battle of Hunayn:** 'Then Allah sent down His tranquility upon His Messenger and upon the believers...' The structure of expression shows that what occurred occurred as a warning and reminder. After this warning, Allah made the Muslims' اکھڑے ہوئے feet firm, and He did this by sending down tranquility and resolve upon His Messenger and his sincere companions, and their tranquility became the cause of producing tranquility in others. The details of this battle recorded in the books of history and biography show that when the army fled, only the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) remained firm in his place with a few devoted companions. Ultimately, his resolve and steadfastness blew spirit into others, the scattered congregation gathered again, and Allah granted them victory after defeat, and the disbelievers faced the punishment decreed for them. 'Tranquility' (*sakīnah*) means resolve, courage, and steadfastness. 'and sent down soldiers which you did not see' points to the unseen armies of angels who accompany the believers everywhere, though they are not visible to physical eyes.

**Verse 27:** 'Then Allah will accept repentance after that of whom He wills; and Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.'

**Glad Tidings to Those in Anxiety Regarding Their Disbelieving Relatives:** 'Then Allah will accept repentance after that of whom He wills...' points to the fact that after these events, He will grant the facilitation of repentance to those who possess some capacity for it. This is a glad tiding to those who were in anxiety regarding their disbelieving relatives. This subject passed in verse 15. This glad tiding is also expressed in clear words in Surah An-Nasr.

**The Final Instruction of Disassociation: Prohibiting the Polytheists From Approaching the Sacred Mosque:**

يٰٓاَيُّھَا الَّذِيْنَ اٰمَنُوْٓا اِنَّمَا الْمُشْرِكُوْنَ نَجَسٌ فَلَا يَقْرَبُوا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ بَعْدَ عَامِهِمْ هٰذَا

This is the final instruction of the declaration of disassociation. He stated: These polytheists are impure (*naǧas*); therefore, after this year, they must not approach the Sacred Mosque. 'Impurity' refers to that impurity of beliefs whose details have been explained in other places. After the Conquest of Mecca, the polytheists' dominance over the Kaaba was ended. But up to the Hajj of 9 AH, they were granted respite to perform the Hajj, performing their pagan rites and carrying out commercial trade. But on the occasion of that Hajj, alongside the general announcement of disassociation, this command was announced: for the future, polytheists must not come to Hajj. This served as an introduction to the Farewell Pilgrimage of 10 AH: when the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) performed the Hajj, the center of Abraham would be completely pure of all traces of polytheism and disbelief.

**Eliminating the Fear of Commercial Decline:** 'And if you fear privation, Allah will enrich you from His bounty if He wills.' *'Aylah* means poverty and privation. Here, it is used in the sense of commercial and economic loss. We explained elsewhere that while Hajj was a great religious worship, it was also highly important from the commercial and economic aspect. Goods from outside would reach Mecca, and merchants from outside would buy Mecca's goods. In the times of ignorance, they gave such significance to this commercial aspect that the Quraysh invented the rule of intercalation (*nasī'*) to keep the Hajj in a specific season, so the change of seasons would not affect the commercial trade. Now, in the Islamic era, when the announcement was made that the polytheists could not come to Hajj, those engaged in commercial life felt anxious. They assumed that blocking the polytheists would affect trade and business, making the Muslims' economic state worse. To satisfy them, He stated: Do not worry about economic decline. If Allah wills, He will enrich you from His bounty. This promise was fulfilled, and fulfilled in such a manner that the trade of Mecca was not affected for even a single day by this restriction; rather, after some time, the treasures of Egypt, Syria, Rome, and Persia reached the treasury of Islam, and Allah enriched the Muslims so much that people wandered in the streets of Medina with zakat, but found no one to accept it.

**True Value Belongs to Spiritual Values:** 'if He wills. Indeed, Allah is Knowing and Wise' clarifies that this depends on the will, knowledge, and wisdom of Allah. Ease or hardship is in God's authority, not man's. For this reason, the core reliance must be on Allah, whose every action is based on knowledge and wisdom. This verse shows that in Islam, true value belongs to spiritual and faithful values. Political and economic interests are subject to them. For spiritual values, economic interests can be sacrificed, but faith cannot be sacrificed for the sake of the belly and body.

The upcoming subject (Verses 29 to 35): Next, He commanded Jihad against the People of the Book—the Jews and Christians—declaring a clear and decisive policy regarding them, just as He did regarding the polytheists: if they wish to live in the Islamic state, they can do so only as subjects (*dhimmi*). Their breach of treaties and conspiracies were detailed in Al-Anfal. Here, He pointed to several of their beliefs and character traits, showing that as a religious group, they are now a useless presence, deserving to face the punishment for their deeds. Recite the verses:

**The Crimes of the People of the Book and the Command Regarding Them:**

قَاتِلُوا الَّذِيْنَ لَا يُؤْمِنُوْنَ بِاللّٰهِ وَلَا بِالْيَوْمِ الْاٰخِرِ وَلَا يُحَرِّمُوْنَ مَا حَرَّمَ اللّٰهُ وَرَسُوْلُهٗ وَلَا يَدِيْنُوْنَ دِيْنَ الْحَقِّ مِنَ الَّذِيْنَ اُوْتُوا الْكِتٰبَ حَتّٰي يُعْطُوا الْجِزْيَةَ عَنْ يَّدٍ وَّهُمْ صٰغِرُوْنَ 

The details of the religious and political crimes of the People of the Book have been explained in Al-Baqarah, Ali 'Imran, Al-Ma'idah, and Al-Anfal. Although they claimed faith—indeed, considered themselves the sole custodians of the religion—those Surahs proved that they believed neither in Allah, the Last Day, nor the Shariah. Believing in Allah requires that it be on Allah's terms, but their belief was on their desires and innovations. By inventing pagan beliefs, they negated God; by declaring themselves a beloved and forgiven nation, they abolished the Last Day; and by making lawful what Allah and His Messenger prohibited, they made the Shariah null and void. Then, when Allah, in accordance with His promise, sent the true religion through His final Messenger, they not only refused to accept it but exerted all their power to oppose it, secretly conspiring against it. He stated: these corrupters no longer deserve any further respite. Fight them until they submit to pay the tax (*jizyah*) and are willing to live as subjects under your authority.

**The Meaning of 'An Yadin':** 'until they give the jizyah willingly (*ʿan yadin*) while they are subdued.' *Yad* literally means hand, but it is also used in the sense of authority, dominance, and power. That is: their paying the tax must be under your dominance and authority. Fight them to crush their power, so they submit to pay the tax with their hands bound.

'while they are subdued' (*wa-hum ṣāġirūna*)—meaning: they accept your authority and subject status, and count it as a blessing.

**The Status of the Jizyah:** The *jizyah* is a tax laid upon every mature, earning male as a compensation for the security granted to their lives, property, and families under the Islamic state. Its quantity varied based on the status and capacity of individuals, and it allowed for many exemptions and concessions. Its details are in our book *The Islamic State*, within the discussion of the rights of non-Muslims.

**The Reason for the Difference Between the People of the Book and the Polytheists:** A clear difference is felt between the treatment of the People of the Book and that of the polytheists. Regarding the polytheists, the command was: do not leave them until they repent and accept Islam; but regarding the People of the Book, instruction was given to grant them security upon paying the tax. This difference is because the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) was sent directly to the polytheists of Arabia. He was sent from among them, the Book was revealed in their language, they were His primary addressees, and He completed the proof against them. After this preparation, no further respite was allowed for them. Consequently, the polytheists of Arabia could not be made subjects (*dhimmi*); but other non-Muslims were given the opportunity to live in the Islamic state as subjects.

**The Ruling on Other Non-Muslims:** Although the ruling here relates to the People of the Book—the Jews and Christians—during the era of the Companions, it was decided that the same ruling applies to other non-Muslims. Thus, the Magians were treated in the same manner as the People of the Book, as they were declared similar to them. If jurists differ on this, it is in details, which are present in the books of jurisprudence.

**The Ruling on the Conquered and the Allies:** It should be kept in mind that this ruling relates to those conquered by force—namely: those who fought the Islamic state and were forced to submit after defeat. Those subjects who entered into treaties of peace and alliance on their own are different. With them, the Islamic state will deal in accordance with the treaty entered into. If they insist on paying the same financial duties as the Muslims, the state may enter into a treaty on this condition. In other words, if they feel humiliation in paying the *jizyah*, they may be exempted from it, and another appropriate form chosen. Breaking such treaties without a valid reason is strictly prohibited in Islam. We discussed these two kinds of subjects in detail in our book *The Islamic State*.

Commentary on Verses 30-31:

**Verse 30:** 'And the Jews say, "Ezra is the son of Allah"; and the Christians say, "The Messiah is the son of Allah."

Ezra is called 'Ezra' in the Torah. His era is said to be around the fifth century BC. In the destruction the Jews suffered at the hands of Nebuchadnezzar, the Torah scriptures were lost; it is said that Ezra compiled the Torah anew from his memory. It is highly probable that because of this achievement of his, the Jews made him the son of God, so his compiled Torah would receive authority, and if anyone doubted it, they could reply: this is the Torah revealed to Moses, which God had His son, Ezra, compile anew. In the pagan environment of that era, such a claim could be highly useful. I have not found the details of this delusion of the Jews in Jewish literature. But the Quran's style of expression shows that this was a well-known statement among the Jews. If it were not, they would have objected to this statement of the Quran. Under the subsequent verse, where He stated that they took their priests and monks as lords, some raised the question to the Prophet: 'We do not worship the priests and monks'; and the Prophet gave them an answer that silenced them. Similarly, if there were any doubt regarding this statement, they would have raised it, but no such objection is recorded anywhere.

The discussion on making Christ the son of God was detailed in the commentary of Ali 'Imran; see it there.

**Proofs of the People of the Book's Lack of Faith:** This describes the proofs for the statement made in the preceding verse: that they believe neither in Allah nor the Last Day. He stated: since the Jews made Ezra the son of God, and the Christians made Christ His son, their belief in God is not valid. Believing in God requires that He be uniquely one in His Being, attributes, and rights, without partner. 'That is their statement by their mouths'—meaning: these are their own slanders which they invented; God never stated that Ezra or Christ is His son.

**Blind Imitation of Past Nations Among the People of the Book:** 'they imitate the saying of those who disbelieved before.' *Mudāhāt* means imitation and similarity. That is: without understanding, they imitate the statement of those who invented this disbelief before them. We explained in the commentary of Ali 'Imran that the Jews and Christians imitated their pagan contemporary nations in their innovations, but gave them a local coloring using those ambiguous words found in their scriptures—such as 'Father' and 'Son' used for God and Christ in the Gospels. Paul chose these terms to make Christianity acceptable among the Romans and Greeks, developing an entire mythology from them. The words 'Father' and 'Son', as we explained, were used in Hebrew in the sense of 'Lord' and 'Servant' and made their meaning clear; but when Hebrew became a dead language, and the Christians had only translations of the Gospels, the literal meanings became the original meanings. Thus, the Christians of Arabia took 'Father' and 'Son' in their physical sense, because this was the meaning in Arabic. The same occurred in other places: the source of beliefs became the translation, not the original Book. The Quran pointed to this deviation: their past leaders developed this mixture of disbelief, and they are blindly imitating it, paying no heed to those who point to the truth.

'May Allah destroy them; how are they deluded?' *Qātalahumu Allāh* (may Allah destroy them) is a phrase of curse in Arabic. 'how are they deluded' (*annā yu'fakūna*) expresses both wonder and grief, and is appropriate when someone chooses a path contrary to clear realities.

**Verse 31:** 'They have taken their scholars and monks as lords besides Allah, and [also] the Messiah, son of Mary. And they were not commanded except to worship one God; there is no deity except Him. Exalted is He above whatever they associate with Him.'

**The Meaning of Taking Scholars and Monks as Lords:** 'They have taken their scholars and monks as lords besides Allah, and [also] the Messiah, son of Mary.' *Ahbār* is the plural of *hibr*, which is used for the jurists of the Jews. *Ruhbān* is the plural of *rāhib*, which is used for the ascetics of the Christians. The reality of taking them as lords is explained in a famous narration from Adi bin Hatim, who said to the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him): 'The Jews and Christians do not worship their scholars and monks.' The Prophet replied: 'Is it not that whatever they declare prohibited, they accept as prohibited, and whatever they declare permissible, they accept as permissible?' He replied: 'Yes, that is so.' The Prophet stated: 'That is taking them as lords and worshiping them.'

**The Delusions Regarding the Messiah, Son of Mary:** 'and [also] the Messiah, son of Mary'—He mentioned Jesus separately to point to all those delusions the Christians invented regarding him: he was taken as a lord, and indeed as the son of God and as God Himself. 'And they were not commanded except to worship one God; there is no deity except Him. Exalted is He above whatever they associate with Him'—that is: they chose this polytheism despite being commanded in the Torah and the Gospel to worship only the One God. References to this were cited in Ali 'Imran.

**The Meaning of 'Subhanahu' (Exalted is He):** *Subhānahu* is used for purification, but it also carries a clear logical proof for monotheism. That is: associating anything with God that possesses attributes contrary to His divine attributes is contrary to reason, because it negates His established attributes. This was discussed in detail in its place.

Commentary on Verses 32-33:

**Verse 32:** 'They want to extinguish the light of Allah with their mouths, but Allah refuses except to perfect His light, although the disbelievers dislike it.'

**Opposing the Truth Through Slanders:** 'They want to extinguish...' In verses 29-30 above, He stated that Allah revealed His true religion to extract them from deviation, but they are not ready to follow it, and are opposing it using those statements which are merely from their mouths, without any divine proof or support from reason and nature. Now, He described this reality in a metaphor: opposing the true religion of Allah using their slanders is like someone attempting to extinguish the sun with their breath, taking it for a clay lamp. He stated: they are attempting this, but Allah has decided to perfect His light, and it will shine like the sun over the horizon, and His decision will be accomplished despite the disbelievers' opposition. This verse will be discussed in Surah As-Saff, where we will highlight several of its aspects.

**Verse 33:** 'It is He who has sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion, although the polytheists dislike it.'

**Glad Tidings of the Victory of the True Religion:** 'It is He who has sent His Messenger with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it over all religion...' The concept of this verse was discussed under Surah Al-Baqarah 192 and Al-Anfal 39. We explained there that the primary purpose of the Prophet's sending was to purify the Sanctuary from all traces of polytheism and disbelief, leaving no other religion dominant there except the true religion, so the center of Abraham would become a source of guidance and light for the entire world. The same is stated here: just as the People of the Book cannot extinguish God's lamp with their breath, the polytheists of Arabia cannot subdue this religion; rather, it will dominate over every religion on this land despite their opposition.

Commentary on Verses 34-35:

**Verse 34:** 'O you who have believed, indeed many of the scholars and the monks devour the wealth of people unjustly and avert [them] from the way of Allah. And those who hoard gold and silver and spend it not in the way of Allah - give them tidings of a painful punishment.'

**The Crimes of the People of the Book Regarding Creation:** 'O you who have believed, indeed many of the scholars and the monks devour the wealth of people unjustly and avert [them] from the way of Allah.' Above, He described their crimes regarding the rights of the Creator; now, He described their crimes regarding the rights of creation, showing that they are now a useless presence, deserving to face the punishment. Note that He exposed the character of their scholars and monks rather than the common people, showing that when the standard-bearers of reform are corrupt, what is there to say of the common people, and what hope remains of their reform? Reform is achieved through the scholars; when they become worshipers of wealth, through whom will reform be achieved?

**The Love of Wealth Among the Scholars of the People of the Book:** Among the Jews, the offices of judgment and verdicts were in the hands of their scholars, and among the Christians, the priests were authorized to issue certificates of salvation. Furthermore, they had made the collection of charity and zakat their own property; hence, broad doors were open to them to gather wealth unjustly. They also openly practiced usury. The Quran points to the fact that they considered the wealth of non-Israelites as lawful for themselves. Jesus rebuked them in highly severe words for their love of wealth: seeing the administrative corruption of the Temple, he stated: 'you have made my Father's house a den of thieves'; he also stated: 'you pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin, but have omitted the weightier matters of the law.'

**The Scholars as Highwaymen:** 'and avert [them] from the way of Allah' points to the reality that they are doing the exact opposite of their primary duty. The scholars are commanded to show people the path of God, but they spend all their capacity in blocking people from the path of God, becoming, in the words of Jesus, highwaymen and robbers. In Al-Baqarah, their conspiracies to block people from Islam were detailed.

**The Reality of Spending (Infaq) and its Blessings:** 'And those who hoard gold and silver and spend it not in the way of Allah - give them tidings of a painful punishment.' Although this points to those worshipers of wealth mentioned above, it is expressed in a general form: whoever hoards wealth and does not spend it in the cause of Allah, give them the glad tiding of a painful punishment. This teaches that wealth is not for hoarding, but for spending in His cause. 'The cause of Allah' (*sabīl Allāh*), as explained elsewhere, refers to all charitable avenues described in the Quran and Hadith. It should noted that He did not state: 'those who hoard wealth and do not pay its zakat'; rather, spending in the cause of Allah (*infāq fī sabīli Allāh*) is different from paying zakat. Every owner of wealth faces two demands: paying its zakat, and spending it in His cause instead of hoarding it. The first demand is legal, and the Islamic state is authorized to collect it by force if needed. The second demand is left to the choice of the owner, but his rank and status before God depend on this voluntary spending. Through this spending, as explained in Al-Baqarah, faith receives strength; through it, wisdom is granted; through it, the light of the heart grows. If an owner of wealth remains indifferent to the orphans and needy of his neighborhood, or to the tasks of calling to Islam, establishing the religion, and Jihad, he is not absolved of responsibility even if he paid the legal zakat. Ahead in this Surah, those hypocrites who considered spending in the cause of Allah as a fine will be mentioned; the Quran declared their love of wealth as proof of their hypocrisy, and threatened them in severe words. This threat was not for failing to pay zakat, as they paid it anyway; their crime was avoiding spending for Jihad despite being rich, and avoiding spending for Jihad is a sign of hypocrisy, and indeed a grave hypocrisy.

**The Wealth of the Companions:** Some use the wealth of certain Companions as proof that gathering wealth is permissible alongside paying zakat, but this is incorrect. The wealth of those Companions was of a commercial and economic nature. Investing and increasing capital in lawful business is not hoarding (*kanz*), but is earning, which is a liked deed in Islam. If a person invests in lawful business, earns through lawful ways, avoids both extravagance and miserliness to spend on his needs, pays zakat, and spends his surplus in the cause of Allah with liberality, he is a true servant of society and accepted before God. Among the Companions, Hazrat Umar Al-Faruq and Hazrat Usman Al-Ghani were such; the benefits the Muslims received from Usman's wealth are undeniable. How can it be assumed that this generous man turned to hoarding wealth at the end of his life?

**The Difference Between Zakat and Spending (Infaq):** But remember that this spending is voluntary and based on choice, not legal and compulsory like zakat. And its blessings lie in this choice. In an Islamic society, this is desired of every owner of wealth, but voluntarily, not by force. It is the duty of the state to prevent the disease of hoarding wealth, encouraging the spirit of spending through its educational and motivational means. The most effective way is that the leaders of the state choose a moderate standard of life, encouraging others to do so, while discouraging those tendencies that incite people to compete in high standards of life.

Commentary on Verse 35:

يَوْمَ يُحْمٰى عَلَيْهَا فِيْ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ فَتُكْوٰي بِهَا جِبَاهُهُمْ وَجُنُوْبُهُمْ وَظُهُوْرُهُمْ ۭهٰذَا مَا كَنَزْتُمْ لِاَنْفُسِكُمْ فَذُوْقُوْا مَا كُنْتُمْ تَكْنِزُوْنَ 

**The End of Hoarding Wealth:** 'The Day it will be heated in the fire of Hell and with it their foreheads, their flanks, and their backs will be branded...'—that is: the wealth hoarded by avoiding the cause of Allah will become a brand on the forehead and a wound on the flank and back on the Day of Resurrection. In hoarding wealth, two things play a major role: the desire to stand high among peers, and the desire for personal comfort. He stated: those who hoard wealth for pride and exultation will find it branding their foreheads; and those who avoid spending to preserve their comfort on soft beds and carpets will find it wounding their flanks and backs.

After commanding war against the polytheists and the People of the Book, He emphasized in the subsequent two verses the respect for the four sacred months, which is a further emphasis of the instruction given in verse 5: 'when the sacred months have passed, then fight...' Along with emphasizing their respect, He corrected the innovation of intercalation (*nasī'*) which the polytheists of Arabia had adopted from the People of the Book. We mentioned previously that to secure their commercial interests, the Arabs had developed a calendar based on the leap-year rule, keeping the sacred months in a specific season. This innovation removed the lunar months from their original places. Since the lunar months are the original divine months, designated for Hajj and Umrah since Abraham's time, it was necessary to correct this innovation now that the task of renewing Abraham's religion was in its final stage, so the sacred months and the rites of Hajj would return to their original places. Consequently, the Farewell Pilgrimage fell exactly on the date which was its original lunar and Abrahimi date. In this light, recite the verses:

اِنَّ عِدَّةَ الشُّهُوْرِ عِنْدَ اللّٰهِ اثْنَا عَشَرَ شَهْرًا فِيْ كِتٰبِ اللّٰهِ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمٰوٰتِ وَالْاَرْضَ مِنْهَآ اَرْبَعَةٌ حُرُمٌ ۭذٰلِكَ الدِّيْنُ الْقَيِّمُ ڏ فَلَا تَظْلِمُوْا فِيْهِنَّ اَنْفُسَكُمْ وَقَاتِلُوا الْمُشْرِكِيْنَ كَاۗفَّةً كَمَا يُقَاتِلُوْنَكُمْ كَاۗفَّةً ۭ وَاعْلَمُوْٓا اَنَّ اللّٰهَ مَعَ الْمُتَّقِيْنَ 

**The Divine Calendar:** 'Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve months in the register of Allah [from] the day He created the heavens and the earth; of these, four are sacred.' This describes the significance of the lunar months: these are the cosmic, divine months, because He has established a highly firm arrangement in the universe for their definition, which clearly shows the calculation of days, months, and years to even the most unlettered person: 'and He determined for it phases - that you might know the number of years and account.' (Yunus: 5). This is the divine calendar preserved in the register of Allah, which has been in use since the day He created the heavens and the earth. This points to the fact that no such cosmic witness exists for those calendars developed by humans. This divine calendar appears on the horizon every month, showing the calculation of days, months, and years through its changing phases, and no error ever occurs in it. 'in the register of Allah' (*fī kitābi Allāhi*)—meaning: in the divine law. In it, it was decreed that a year consists of twelve lunar months, of which four are sacred. This points to the fact that adding to or reducing these twelve months, or removing them from their places, causes a disruption in this divine calendar, which is contrary to the will of its Creator.

**The True Religious Calendar:** 'That is the correct religion'—meaning: this is the correct, natural, and cosmic calendar, just as Islam is the correct religion because its witness exists in the universe. It should be kept in mind that just as seasons are significant in farming, times, days, and years are significant in religious matters. Just as farming out of season is fruitless, performing prayer, fasting, or Hajj out of time is fruitless. For this reason, it was necessary that the calendar under which religious worships and rites are regulated also carries a religious status. From this aspect, this calendar is termed 'the correct religion' (*al-dīnu al-qayyimu*), because it is a highly important part of the religion. Making alterations in it is equivalent to distorting the religion.

**Respecting the Sacred Months:** 'so do not wrong yourselves during them.' That is: commit no action contrary to their sanctity, nor make any alteration in them. If you initiate fighting in them, you will harm yourselves, not God, because God made them sacred for your own benefit, and if you make alterations in them, you will deprive yourselves of the blessings He placed in them.

**Command to Fight the Polytheists Collectively:** 'And fight against the polytheists collectively as they fight against you collectively. And know that Allah is with those who fear Him.' That is: while respecting the sanctity of these months, fight these polytheists, and let this fighting be on a collective basis. Let no distinction be made based on relative-ship, tribal bias, personal, commercial, or economic interests. Ignore all such relationships to declare war against all polytheists, because their conduct toward you is of this nature. They are your enemies collectively: 'they do not regard concerning a believer consanguinity or covenant.' Just so, be their enemies collectively, and kill them wherever you find them. However, respect the sacred months, initiating no fighting in them, though defensive war, as explained in Surah Al-Baqarah, is permissible in them. Remember that Allah is on the side of those who respect His limits.
